summary.intent: Address Codex automated review feedback on a Rust autocomplete component (PR #685 in fogodev/ars-ui), fixing state machine bugs around controlled clear, filtering, disabled prop sync, loading-state restoration, and stale selected keys.
summary.outcome: All five review issues were fixed in commit bcf6b07f and a subsequent commit. Six targeted regression tests were added, the fast CI gate passed all 10 steps, and the changes were pushed to the PR. Three prior Codex review threads were replied to and resolved via GraphQL.

repo: The repo uses a custom CI profile 'cargo xci --profile fast' covering rustfmt, check, clippy, and nextest as a pre-push gate.
Codex review is triggered via '@codex review' PR comment and its progress is tracked by GitHub reactions (eyes = in progress) and GraphQL review thread queries.
PR review thread replies require GraphQL mutations (addPullRequestReviewThreadReply / resolveReviewThread); the REST reply endpoint does not work for this repo's comment IDs.
Coverage is measured with cargo-llvm-cov targeting specific modules (selection/autocomplete).
The autocomplete state machine has distinct Idle/focused/unfocused contexts; Clear must target Idle when input is unfocused to preserve consistent state semantics.
IME composition guard: input keydown handlers must exit early during active IME composition to avoid interfering with arrow/Home/End/Enter/Escape keys.

code.finding: next_state_for_loading can be a const fn — Clippy enforces this.
Uncontrolled selection must refresh visible_keys and live-region output against the selected label after updating input_value.
Five state/contract gaps identified by Codex: controlled clear/filtering, disabled prop sync, loading-state restoration, and stale selected keys — all real bugs, not reviewer misunderstandings.

workflow: Run red tests first (TDD): add failing regression tests for each review point before patching logic to confirm the tests actually catch the bugs.
cargo +nightly fmt --all is required before the fast gate passes; rustfmt diffs on multiline assertions are a common friction point.
Poll Codex review state with GraphQL reviewThreads query filtered by author login matching 'codex' and isResolved==false, rather than relying on the compact gh pr checks view.
Workflow run jobs API (gh api repos/.../actions/jobs/...) gives more reliable status than gh pr checks when the checks view lags.

friction: REST PR comment reply endpoint rejected the comment IDs, requiring a pivot to GraphQL mutations for both replies and thread resolution.
Codex review took 10+ minutes to produce results after the '@codex review' trigger, requiring multiple polling loops with sleep intervals.
gh pr checks view showed stale/sparse data during early CI queue expansion; had to use the workflow run and job APIs directly to confirm actual job status.
rustfmt failed the fast gate on multiline assertion formatting in test code, requiring a separate fmt pass and re-run.
Clippy flagged next_state_for_loading as needing const fn, requiring an additional patch after the main logic fixes.
open_items: Another Codex review pass was triggered on the final push (bcf6b07f fix commit); results of that review are not yet captured in the transcript.
GitHub remote unit tests were still pending at the end of the session — no confirmation of remote test suite pass for the final commit.
Section/separator collection builder usage was investigated but no changes were made — may be a related area worth revisiting.
Coverage report for selection/autocomplete was generated but the specific coverage percentage was not recorded or evaluated against a threshold.
